revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{日本の古墳 |名称= 美園古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府八尾市美園 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長７ｍ |築造年代= 4世...'
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称= 美園古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府八尾市美園 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長７ｍ |築造年代= 4世紀末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=高床式家型埴輪 壷形土器 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''美園古墳'''（みそのづかこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]([[方墳]])。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の遺構の１つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家型[[埴輪]]等が出土している。
== 概要== 1981年、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての複合遺跡である美園遺跡の発掘調査で、[[古墳時代]]の遺構面で一辺７，2ｍの削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2ｍほどの周濠から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壷形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]] [[高床式]]住居（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70ｃｍ、棟の最大長75ｃｍ、桁行最大長５５ｃｍ、梁行の最大長４５ｃｍであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布され痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3，5ｃｍ高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれそれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の家を忠実に再現している可能性が大きいとされる。 もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60ｃｍ、桁行最大長は約42ｃｍ、梁行最大長は約38ｃｍであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壷形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10ｃｍほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。 これらの家形埴輪2個と壷形土器の内３点は重要文化財に指定され、現在は[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]に所蔵されている。 ==参考文献 == *『美園』 本文編 財団法人大阪文化財ｾﾝﾀｰ 1985年 401項‐411項
==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]
[[category:古墳]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 美園古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府八尾市美園 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長７ｍ |築造年代= 4世紀末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=高床式家型埴輪 壷形土器 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''美園古墳'''（みそのづかこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]([[方墳]])。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の遺構の１つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家型[[埴輪]]等が出土している。

==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]
[[category:古墳]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 美園古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府八尾市美園 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長 7ｍ |築造年代= [[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=高床式家型埴輪 壷形土器 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''美園古墳'''（みそのづかこふん）とは、[[大阪府]] [[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]([[方墳]])。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の遺構の１つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家型[[埴輪]]等が出土している。

{{日本の古墳 |名称= 美園古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府八尾市美園 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長 7ｍ |築造年代= [[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=高床式家型埴輪 壷形土器 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の遺構の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家型[[埴輪]]等が出土している。
== 概要 == {{jdate|1981}}、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての複合遺跡である美園遺跡の発掘調査で、[[古墳時代]]の遺構面で一辺7.2mの削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2mほどの周濠から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壷形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]] [[高床式]]住居（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70cm、棟の最大長75cm、桁行最大長55cm、梁行の最大長45cmであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布され痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5cm高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれそれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の家を忠実に再現している可能性が大きいとされる。 もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壷形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。 これらの家形埴輪2個と壷形土器の内3点は重要文化財に指定され、現在は[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]に所蔵されている。
==参考文献 == *『美園』 本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401項‐411項

==参考文献 == *『美園』 本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401項‐411項
==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]

== 家形埴輪と壷形埴輪== 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壷形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]] [[高床式]]住居（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70cm、棟の最大長75cm、桁行最大長55cm、梁行の最大長45cmであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布され痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5cm高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれそれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の家を忠実に再現している可能性が大きいとされる。
もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壷形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。
これらの家形埴輪2個と壷形土器の内3点は重要文化財に指定され、現在は[[近つ飛鳥博物館]]に所蔵されている。
==参考文献 == *『美園』 本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401項‐411項
==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン]
{{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=美園古墳 |画像= |所在地=大阪府八尾市美園 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長 7m　 |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=高床式家形埴輪　壷形土器 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の遺構の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家形[[埴輪]]等が出土している。 == 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての複合遺跡である美園遺跡の発掘調査で、[[古墳時代]]の遺構面で一辺7.2mの削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2mほどの周濠から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。 == 家形埴輪と壷形埴輪== [[ファイル:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|出土家形埴輪

revision.comment: 重文埴輪の所有者が国であることを明示。「壺」を印刷標準字体に。

{{日本の古墳 |名称=美園古墳 |画像= |所在地=大阪府八尾市美園 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長 7m　 |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者= |出土品=高床式家形埴輪　壷形土器 |史跡指定= |特記事項=埋没古墳 }} '''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の遺構の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家形[[埴輪]]等が出土している。 == 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての複合遺跡である美園遺跡の発掘調査で、[[古墳時代]]の遺構面で一辺7.2mの削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2mほどの周濠から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。 == 家形埴輪と壺形埴輪== [[ファイル:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|thumb|180px|right|{{center|出土家形埴輪
（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示。}}]] 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壺形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]] [[高床式]]住居（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70cm、棟の最大長75cm、桁行最大長55cm、梁行の最大長45cmであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布され痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5cm高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれぞれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の家を忠実に再現している可能性が大きいとされる。 もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壺形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。 これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形土器の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 平成7年6月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}。 ==参考文献 == *『美園』　本文編　財団法人大阪文化財センター　1985年　401項‐411項 ==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]] ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン] {{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: 地図を加えてみました。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=美園古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] |画像キャプション =家形埴輪（国の[[重要文化財]]、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示） |所在地=大阪府八尾市美園 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長7m |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=高床式家形埴輪 壺形埴輪 |史跡指定= |有形文化財=家形埴輪・壺形埴輪:1995年（平成7年）国指定<ref name=Yao1>{{Cite web|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=54|title=美園古墳 家形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}}</ref><ref name=Yao2>{{Cite web|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=55|title=美園古墳 壺形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}}</ref> |特記事項=埋没古墳 |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=美園古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord2|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }}
'''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の[[遺構]]の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家形[[埴輪]]や壺形埴輪が出土している。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての[[複合遺跡]]である美園遺跡の[[発掘調査]]で、[[古墳時代]]の遺構面で一辺7.2mの削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2mほどの周溝から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。
== 家形埴輪と壺形埴輪== 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壺形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]] [[高床式]]住居（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70cm、棟の最大長75cm、桁行最大長55cm、梁行の最大長45cmであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布され痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5cm高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれぞれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の家を忠実に再現している可能性が大きいとされる。
もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壺形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。
これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形土器の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。
== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}</ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401項‐411項
==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/SearchDetail.do?heritageId=213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン]
{{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壺形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。

==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401項‐411項

==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン]
{{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=美園古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] |画像キャプション =家形埴輪（国の[[重要文化財]]、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示） |所在地=大阪府八尾市美園 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長7m |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=高床式家形埴輪 壺形埴輪 |史跡指定= |有形文化財=家形埴輪・壺形埴輪:1995年（平成7年）国指定<ref name=Yao1>{{Cite web|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=54|title=美園古墳 家形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}}</ref><ref name=Yao2>{{Cite web|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=55|title=美園古墳 壺形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}}</ref> |特記事項=埋没古墳 |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=美園古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }}
'''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から発掘された埋没古墳の[[遺構]]の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の家形[[埴輪]]や壺形埴輪が出土している。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての[[複合遺跡]]である美園遺跡の[[発掘調査]]で、[[古墳時代]]の遺構面で一辺7.2mの削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2mほどの周溝から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。
== 家形埴輪と壺形埴輪== 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壺形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]] [[高床住居]]（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70cm、棟の最大長75cm、桁行最大長55cm、梁行の最大長45cmであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布され痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5cm高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれぞれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の家を忠実に再現している可能性が大きいとされる。
もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壺形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。
これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形土器の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。
== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}</ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401項‐411項
==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン]

もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]倉庫（平面2間×2間）を表現しており入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、倉庫を表現していると思われる。高さ31cm、棟の長さ60cm、桁行最大長は約42cm、梁行最大長は約38cmであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。壺形土器は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10cmほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。
これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形土器の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。
== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}</ref>。
==脚注== {{Reflist}}
==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年 401‐411頁
==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]]
==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン]

'''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から検出された埋没古墳の[[遺構]]の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の[[形象埴輪|家形埴輪]]や壺形埴輪が出土している。
== 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての[[複合遺跡]]である美園遺跡の[[発掘調査]]で、[[古墳時代]]の[[遺構面]]で一辺7.2メートルの、墳丘を削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2メートルほどの周溝から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。
== 家形埴輪と壺形埴輪== 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壺形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]]の[[高床建物]]（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70センチメートル、棟の最大長75センチメートル、桁行最大長55センチメートル、梁行の最大長45センチメートルであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布された痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5センチメートル高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれぞれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の[[住居]]を忠実に再現している可能性が高いとされる。
もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]の建物（平面2間×2間）を表現しており、入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、[[倉庫]]を表現していると考えられている。高さ31センチメートル、棟の長さ60センチメートル、桁行最大長は約42センチメートル、梁行最大長は約38センチメートルであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。
壺形埴輪は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10センチメートルほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から[[壺]]ではなく埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。
これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形埴輪の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。
== 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定<ref>{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}</ref>。

{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年　401‐411頁 ==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]] ==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン] {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=美園古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] |画像キャプション =家形埴輪（国の[[重要文化財]]）
（[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示） |所在地=大阪府八尾市美園 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長7m　 |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=[[高床建物]]の家形埴輪 壺形埴輪 |史跡指定= |有形文化財=家形埴輪・壺形埴輪:1995年（平成7年）国指定{{Cite web|和書|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=54|title=美園古墳 家形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}}{{Cite web|和書|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=55|title=美園古墳 壺形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}} |特記事項=埋没古墳 |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=美園古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}}}} '''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から検出された埋没古墳の[[遺構]]の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の[[形象埴輪|家形埴輪]]や壺形埴輪が出土している。 == 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての[[複合遺跡]]である美園遺跡の[[発掘調査]]で、[[古墳時代]]の[[遺構面]]で一辺7.2メートルの、墳丘を削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2メートルほどの周溝から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。 == 家形埴輪と壺形埴輪== 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壺形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]]の[[高床建物]]（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70センチメートル、棟の最大長75センチメートル、桁行最大長55センチメートル、梁行の最大長45センチメートルであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布された痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5センチメートル高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれぞれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の[[住居]]を忠実に再現している可能性が高いとされる。 もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]の建物（平面2間×2間）を表現しており、入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、[[倉庫]]を表現していると考えられている。高さ31センチメートル、棟の長さ60センチメートル、桁行最大長は約42センチメートル、梁行最大長は約38センチメートルであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。 壺形埴輪は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10センチメートルほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から[[壺]]ではなく埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。 これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形埴輪の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。 美園古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|家形埴輪
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年　401‐411頁 ==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]] ==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン] {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | b..."
revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=Location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao]] [[Category:Square tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

{{Infobox kofun | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])

Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao]] [[Category:Square tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:美園古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox kofun | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Yao]] [[Category:Square tumuli]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:美園古墳]]

One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:Misono Kofun Excavated House-shaped Haniwa.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Square tumuli]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:new pages]][[ja:[[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]] [[Category:Pages where the interlanguage links were botched]][[Category:original pages]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:dd]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:pp]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pp]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:dd]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
