{{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Misono ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == See ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, pp. 401–411. == See {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean burial mound | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat aqqroximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (aqqroximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (aqqroximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, qq. 401–411. == See Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat aqqroximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (aqqroximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (aqqroximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411.== See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map2 = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-scale house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun revision.comment: another mass replace to make the parameters work correctly
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Cultural Heritage Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-typed haniwa, jar-typed haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-map_size house-typed and jar-typed {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important Cultural Properties of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-typed and Jar-typed Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-typed haniwa and over 25 jar-typed haniwa. One house-typed haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-typed haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-typed haniwa resemble morning-glory-typed cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-typed and one jar-typed) are designated as National Important Cultural Properties, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-typed haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === National Important Cultural Properties === * Two house-typed haniwa and three jar-typed haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-typed and Jar-typed Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Cultural Properties Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-typed and Jar-typed Haniwa from Misono Kofun (other_designation Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important Cultural Properties Database: House-typed and Jar-typed Haniwa from Misono Kofun] revision.comment: trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[Important other_designation of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National Important other_designation, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_designation == === National Important other_designation === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka other_designation Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (other_designation Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National Important other_designation Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun]
