([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | dimensions = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | artifacts = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of artifacts, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designated as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designated as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial_facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, designation1 as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-formed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka other_designation Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (other_designation Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_other_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, other_designation1 as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka other_other_designation Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | occupant = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_other_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, other_designation1 as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka other_designation Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (other_designation Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_other_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, other_designation1 as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka other_designation Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (other_designation Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_other_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, other_designation1 as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_other_designation = National [[Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, other_designation1 as [[[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are other_designation1 as National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == other_designation == === National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka other_designation Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (other_designation Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Misono Kofun]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are other_designation1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building ([[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. ([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]]

([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]]

([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[[[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[Important designation1 (Japan)|Important designation1]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}
