([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=美園古墳 |画像=[[ファイル:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] |画像キャプション =家形埴輪（国の[[重要文化財]]）
（[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]展示） |所在地=大阪府八尾市美園 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} |形状= 方墳 |規模=墳丘長7m　 |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |埋葬施設=不明 |被葬者=不明 |出土品=[[高床建物]]の家形埴輪 壺形埴輪 |史跡指定= |有形文化財=家形埴輪・壺形埴輪:1995年（平成7年）国指定{{Cite web|和書|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=54|title=美園古墳 家形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}}{{Cite web|和書|author=八尾市教育委員会|url=http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=55|title=美園古墳 壺形埴輪|work=八尾市|accessdate=2021-02-02}} |特記事項=埋没古墳 |地図2={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=美園古墳|lat_deg=34|lat_min=38|lat_sec=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=大阪府内の位置}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}}}} '''美園古墳'''（みそのこふん）とは、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]美園4丁目に所在した[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[近畿自動車道]]建設予定地の地下から検出された埋没古墳の[[遺構]]の1つであり、墳丘が古墳として最小規模のものでありながら、[[重要文化財]]に指定された大型の[[形象埴輪|家形埴輪]]や壺形埴輪が出土している。 == 概要 == [[1981年]]（[[昭和]]56年）、近畿自動車道建設予定地内の[[弥生時代]]前期から[[中世]]にかけての[[複合遺跡]]である美園遺跡の[[発掘調査]]で、[[古墳時代]]の[[遺構面]]で一辺7.2メートルの、墳丘を削平された[[方墳]]の遺構が検出された。墳丘のほとんどを削平され、埋葬主体も検出されなかったが、墳丘を囲む幅2メートルほどの周溝から[[埴輪]]類が出土した。 == 家形埴輪と壺形埴輪== 埴輪類は精巧な家形埴輪2点と壺形埴輪25点以上で構成されていた。家形埴輪2点は北側周濠コーナー部分より出土しており、1点は[[入母屋造]]の[[高床建物]]（平面2間×2間）を表現したもので、高さ70センチメートル、棟の最大長75センチメートル、桁行最大長55センチメートル、梁行の最大長45センチメートルであった。外面全体と内部の一部を[[弁柄|ベンガラ]]で赤く塗布された痕跡がある。内部には床部分とそれより、3.5センチメートル高くしたベッド状施設を設け、外面の4面の中央にはそれぞれ、線刻で盾を描いている。それらの盾には鋸歯文による文様が施されていた。この家形埴輪は、かなり精巧に作られており、当時の首長の[[住居]]を忠実に再現している可能性が高いとされる。 もう1点の家形埴輪は[[切妻造]]の建物（平面2間×2間）を表現しており、入口が1箇所認められる他は窓が設けられておらず、[[倉庫]]を表現していると考えられている。高さ31センチメートル、棟の長さ60センチメートル、桁行最大長は約42センチメートル、梁行最大長は約38センチメートルであった。外面はやはりベンガラで塗布されていた。 壺形埴輪は朝顔形円筒埴輪と類似した形状のものが多く、底部には10センチメートルほどの孔が焼成前からあけられており、当初から[[壺]]ではなく埴輪として製作されたものとかんがえられるという。 これらの家形埴輪2個と壺形埴輪の内3点は[[重要文化財]]に指定されている（[[文化庁]]蔵、[[大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館]]保管）。 美園古墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|家形埴輪
{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年　401‐411頁 ==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]] ==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン] *[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 国指定文化財等データベース 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土] {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[美園古墳]] to [[Misono Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{small|[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]附属博物館企画展示時に撮影。}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 家形埴輪2箇・壺形埴輪3箇 大阪府八尾市美園古墳出土（考古資料） - 1995年（平成7年）6月15日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|10118|家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土}}。 ==脚注== {{Reflist}} ==参考文献 == *『美園』本文編 財団法人大阪文化財センター 1985年　401‐411頁 ==関連項目== *[[埴輪]] *[[長原古墳群]] ==外部リンク== *[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土 文化遺産オンライン] *[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 国指定文化財等データベース 家形埴輪／壺形埴輪／大阪府八尾市美園町美園古墳出土] {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{デフォルトソート:みそのこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[category:方墳]] [[category:大阪府の重要文化財]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Misono Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=draft}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|12=draft}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=draft}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|12=draft}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao|ja|八尾市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao|qq=}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki Expressway|qq=}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}, Historic Site1 as [[{{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|Irimoya-style|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-style|qq=}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|ko|일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Misono Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]]

([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:八尾市の歴史
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]]

Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[en:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|10=ja|11=奈良県立橿原考古学研究所附属博物館|comment=trying to get the bot to work with these using this|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara Archaeological Institute|qq=}}.}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]

Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]
