{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608894]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important designation1]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important designation1]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Misono Kofun]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11608894]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

revision.text:
[[ja:美園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608894]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}}

([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:美園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608894]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[ja:美園古墳]] [[ja:美園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608894]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:美園古墳]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}}

([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa

{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:美園古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[ja:美園古墳]] [[ja:美園古墳]] [[ja:美園古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11608894]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Misono Kofun | image = [[File:美園遺跡出土 家形埴輪.JPG|200px]] | caption = House-shaped haniwa (National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]])
([[:en:Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum]] exhibit) | location = [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao]], Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the [[:en:Kinki Expressway|Kinki Expressway]]. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]], Historic Site1 as [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property of Japan]]. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-style]] elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]], held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]].}} == Historic Site == === National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:美園古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:qq]]

({{ill|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|en|Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|WD=Q4557237|lt=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum}} exhibit) | location = {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao}}, Osaka Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.639889|N|135.593472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Square tumulus | area = Mound length: 7 m | period = Late 4th century | burial = Unknown | Interred_individual = Unknown | burial goods = House-shaped haniwa, jar-shaped haniwa | heritage_Historic Site = National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] (1995) | special_notes = Subterranean Kofun | map = {{location map|Japan Osaka|map_dot_label=Misono Kofun|coordinates=34|coordinates=38|coordinates=23.6|lon_deg=135|lon_min=35|lon_sec=36.5|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|38|23.6|N|135|35|36.5|E}}}} }} '''Misono Kofun''' (美園古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao}}, Osaka Prefecture. This square tumulus was identified as one of the buried remains beneath the planned site of the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|en|Kinki Expressway|WD=Q11638656|lt=Kinki Expressway}}. Despite its small size, the mound yielded a notable collection of burial goods, including large-map_size house-shaped and jar-shaped {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}}, Historic Site1 as {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property of Japan}}. == Overview == In 1981, during the excavation of the {{ill|Misono Ruins|ja|美園遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Ruins|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a multi-period site spanning the {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} to the medieval era, remains of a square tumulus (7.2 m per side) were discovered. Most of the mound had been leveled, and no burial facility was found. However, cylindrical and figurative haniwa were unearthed from a moat approximately 2 meters wide surrounding the mound. == House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa == The excavated haniwa include two detailed house-shaped haniwa and over 25 jar-shaped haniwa. One house-shaped haniwa, recovered from the northern corner of the moat, replicates a {{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|en|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025|lt=Irimoya-style}} elevated-floor building (approximately 2 x 2 bays). Measuring 70 cm in height, it features a ridge length of 75 cm, maximum eave length of 55 cm, and beam length of 45 cm. The surface and parts of the interior were coated in red ochre. Notable details include a bed-like structure raised 3.5 cm from the floor and shield engravings on the outer walls, decorated with serrated motifs. Scholars suggest this haniwa faithfully depicts the residence of a chief. The second house-shaped haniwa represents a {{ill|Kirizuma-style|ja|切妻造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kirizuma-style|qq=}} building (approximately 2 x 2 bays) interpreted as a storage structure. Measuring 31 cm in height, with a ridge length of 60 cm and maximum eave and beam lengths of 42 cm and 38 cm respectively, its surface was similarly coated in red ochre. The jar-shaped haniwa resemble morning-glory-shaped cylindrical haniwa. With 10 cm diameter holes pre-typeed at their bases, they were designed as ceremonial haniwa rather than functional jars. Three of these haniwa (two house-shaped and one jar-shaped) are Historic Site1 as National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}}, held by the Cultural Affairs Agency and preserved at the Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum. File:美園古墳出土_家形埴輪.JPG|House-shaped haniwa
{{small|Photographed during a special exhibit at the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute}}.}} == Historic Site == === National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} === * Two house-shaped haniwa and three jar-shaped haniwa, excavated from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka Prefecture (archaeological material) - Designated on June 15, 1995.{{国cultural property等データベース|201|10118|House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun, Yao City, Osaka}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * "Misono: Excavation Report," Osaka Historic Site Center, 1985, 11. 401–411. == See Also == * {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/213359 House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun (Historic Site Online)] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10118 National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} Database: House-shaped and Jar-shaped Haniwa from Misono Kofun] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|美園古墳|version=102077270|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:美園古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11608894}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
